creator: hrkfdn@gmail.com
text:
When reading (audio) CDs, the CD driver ends up in a FAILURE loop. If this happens for example during boot-time, it won't finish booting FreeBSD because it will never get any further than reading the CD label.
I started having this error with the first 7.0 releases, if I remember correctly. The drive works fine with other OSes, so I'm pretty sure it's not a hardware issue. It also doesn't matter, whether the CDs are copy-protected or not. The CDs have no scratches whatsoever.

This is what dmesg spits out when it happens:
acd0: TIMEOUT - READ_CD retrying (1 retry left)
acd0: TIMEOUT - READ_CD retrying (0 retries left)
acd0: FAILURE - READ_CD timed out
acd0: TIMEOUT - READ_CD retrying (1 retry left)
acd0: TIMEOUT - READ_CD retrying (0 retries left)
acd0: FAILURE - READ_CD timed out
acd0: TIMEOUT - READ_CD retrying (1 retry left)

acd0: FAILURE - READ_CD timed out
acd0: TIMEOUT - READ_CD retrying (1 retry left)
acd0: TIMEOUT - READ_CD retrying (0 retries left)
acd0: FAILURE - READ_CD timed out
acd0: TIMEOUT - READ_CD retrying (1 retry left)
